message: Stage -1.2 (single-artifact) authored angle=0 v1 mode=cold-start → proto_core.json

Stage 0-SOLVE surfaced proposed change(s) — review before applying:
1 STATEMENT change(s) — narrow a too-strong claim, never weaken to ease the proof:
  - thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint [narrow]: The last equality clause of the current theorem is stronger than what follows from the frozen primitives. Qmsm_a_plus is defined only as the pointwise supremum of feasible MSM quantiles. The core does not state that this pointwise envelope is generated by one weight w in W_a^Gamma(x), nor that it coincides with an MSM mean-maximizing law. Without such a representability or uniqueness theorem, the condition "Qmsm_a_plus lies in the tube" is neither necessary nor sufficient for V_cpl_plus = V_MSM_plus. What the proof does establish is the narrower and honest criterion: equality holds exactly when some MSM mean-maximizing latent law is itself jointly feasible for the tube.
To apply: rewind D-1.2/D0 with the corrected node(s); proposals written under /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec/discovery.
D0 revise loop GATED 1 change(s) for human review:
  - thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint: narrowing a THEOREM headline (thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint) — human review

Stage 0-SOLVE hit 1 GENUINE OPEN GAP(S) — orchestrator guidance requested (provide a direction / paper / reframing via the D0 directive, then re-run; a blind re-solve will not close these):
  - conj:eif-one-step: OPEN = Derive, from the frozen primitives alone, the pathwise derivative of the coupled endpoint map along regular submodels preserving the active face A_a_plus, prove that its canonical gradient is phi_cpl_plus = phi_MSM_plus - phi_tube_plus, and show that the cross-fit estimator Vhat_cpl_plus has an o_p(n^{-1/2}) remainder uniformly over the stated class.
      obstruction: The frozen core names phi_MSM_plus as a published baseline EIF and imposes rate conditions on eta_MSM and fobs_a, but it does not include the baseline pathwise differentiability theorem for V_MSM_plus, an envelope theorem for differentiating the coupled argmax under fixed-face perturbations, a stability result for the estimated contact set, or a stochastic equicontinuity / empirical-process argument controlling the coupled plug-in Chat_a_plus. Ass:regular-contact gives uniqueness of the optimizer and an interior finite-union contact set, but that is not enough by itself to derive the semiparametric derivative or the one-step remainder bound.
      tried: I pushed the available structure as far as it goes. Using thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint and ass:regular-contact, one can isolate the candidate first-order correction coming from perturbations of the active tube face. Matching the inverse-CDF derivative pattern from Firpo-style arguments yields exactly the contact adjustment in def:phi-tube-plus. Combining that with the named baseline score suggests phi_cpl_plus = phi_MSM_plus - phi_tube_plus. But closing the proof requires three missing ingredients that are not derivable from the frozen primitives: (i) a proved baseline gradient result for phi_MSM_plus, (ii) a fixed-face envelope theorem for the coupled optimizer, and (iii) an empirical-process expansion proving that cross-fit nuisance error and contact-set estimation enter only at o_p(n^{-1/2}).
Written to /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec/discovery/pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_open_obligations.json.

Stage 0-SOLVE discharged 1 target(s) across 1 dispatched unit(s) (reused 3 carried member proof(s) + 5 lemma(s)), added 2 lemma(s); Stage 0-RENDER rendered the prose note (deterministic).
Pruned 1 orphan lemma(s) no longer reachable from any result: lem:coupled-endpoint-corrected.
D0 MAXIMALITY CHECKPOINT — the paper is fully proved/discharged. Review whether the WHOLE paper is maximized (no room to improve) BEFORE D0.5; iterate D0 if not, else --resume to proceed to the D0.5 review. (No defect — this is the review gate, not an escalation.)

Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 0 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: Cannot honestly complete an in-place revise because the semiparametric rung is genuinely undischarged from the frozen DAG. The optimizer-attainment/unused-assumption issues at thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint are locally fixable by restating the endpoint through the supremal-value functional C_a^\pm rather than attained argmax laws. But findings at lem:contact-face-derivative, lem:contact-set-second-order, and conj:eif-one-step require real new work beyond note editing: the current core does not prove the baseline MSM gradient theorem, a fixed-face envelope theorem for the coupled optimizer, the weighted-density/contact-face derivative, or the contact-set stability / empirical-process remainder needed for orthogonality and root-n inference. This is already recorded in discovery/pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_open_obligations.json. Under the typed D0 gate every final statement node must remain status="proved"; there is no accepted-open conjecture status, so the only honest repair would be to weaken/drop the inference claim or add new load-bearing results, both forbidden by the revise bounds.
Open findings: argmax_not_discharged@thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint, unused_assumptions@thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint, weighted_quantile_term_dropped@lem:contact-face-derivative, boundary_rate_missing@lem:contact-set-second-order, orthogonality_not_reproduced@conj:eif-one-step, optimizer-attainment-gap@thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint, undeclared-external-dependency@lem:contact-face-derivative, unsupported-second-order-remainder@lem:contact-set-second-order. Provide guidance via the D0 directive (a new direction / a paper to adapt / a reframing) and re-run, or rewind D0/D-1.2.

Stage 0-SOLVE discharged 1 target(s) across 1 dispatched unit(s) (reused 3 carried member proof(s) + 6 lemma(s)), added 0 lemma(s); Stage 0-RENDER rendered the prose note (deterministic).
D0 MAXIMALITY CHECKPOINT — the paper is fully proved/discharged. Review whether the WHOLE paper is maximized (no room to improve) BEFORE D0.5; iterate D0 if not, else --resume to proceed to the D0.5 review. (No defect — this is the review gate, not an escalation.)

Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 0 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: Cannot honestly complete an in-place revise because several must-fix findings require real new work beyond editing the note. The blocking nodes are lem:contact-face-eif-decomposition (eif-derivation-asserted, undeclared_external_imports), lem:chat-plus-consistency (uniform_feasible_class_convergence_missing, argmax-consistency-unsupported, graphical_convergence_gap), and conj:eif-one-step (consistency_claim_not_discharged, inference_rung_partially_discharged). Discharging them from the frozen DAG would require new load-bearing results: a proved baseline MSM pathwise-differentiability/EIF theorem in the DAG or an accepted substrate gate, a fixed-face envelope derivation for the active latent law, and a contact-set/feasible-class stability plus empirical-process remainder proof strong enough to justify plug-in consistency and the claimed inference package. The revise bounds forbid the only note-level escape hatches: adding new boundary-stability/uniform-convergence assumptions or weakening/dropping the inference claim. The attainment-side findings at lem:joint-feasible-extrema-attained / thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint / prop:rect-gap are locally repairable, but the semiparametric rung remains genuinely undischarged, so producing a partial edit would violate the spec.
Open findings: tube_closure_not_reproduced@lem:joint-feasible-extrema-attained, redundant_assumptions@thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint, zero_gap_criterion_needs_attainment@prop:rect-gap, uniform_feasible_class_convergence_missing@lem:chat-plus-consistency, consistency_claim_not_discharged@conj:eif-one-step, lower-tube-closure-gap@lem:joint-feasible-extrema-attained, sharpness-rests-on-unproved-attainment@thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint, eif-derivation-asserted@lem:contact-face-eif-decomposition. Provide guidance via the D0 directive (a new direction / a paper to adapt / a reframing) and re-run, or rewind D0/D-1.2.
